# 💡 Brainstorming Activity: Research Your Final Project Idea with Copilot

⏰ **~10 minutes** · Your first hands-on time in **GitHub Codespaces** + **GitHub Copilot**

This is a quick warm-up to get your hands dirty. You'll use Copilot as a **brainstorming partner**
to explore ideas for your **[Final Video Project](https://bsmp-coders.github.io/#/2026/program/final_projects)**,
then have Copilot help you write it all up in a `my_project_idea.md` file you can keep.

Don't stress about picking the *perfect* idea today — the goal is to practice *thinking out loud with
AI* and get comfortable with the tools.

---

## 🎯 What You'll Do

1. Open **Copilot Chat** for the first time.
2. **Brainstorm** project ideas with Copilot — ask questions, react, refine.
3. Have Copilot help you **create a `my_project_idea.md`** file that captures your best idea.

By the end you'll have a written starting point for your capstone — and you'll have seen how Copilot
can help you *think*, not just *code*.

---

## 🧠 Learn: Why brainstorm with an AI?

A blank page is the hardest part of any project. Copilot is great at helping you **get unstuck**:

- It can suggest ideas when you're not sure where to start.
- It can ask *you* questions to sharpen a vague idea.
- It can help you weigh "is this too big?" or "how would I even build this?"

The trick: treat it like a **conversation**, not a vending machine. React to what it says, push back,
and steer it toward what *you* actually care about. **You** are the one deciding — Copilot is just
helping you think faster.

---

## 🛠️ Build: Brainstorm with Copilot


### Quick Start Checklist (Every Class)

- [ ] Open the lesson repo in **Codespaces** 👉 see details [on using codespace](https://bsmp-coders.github.io/#/students/Getting-Started?id=_2-create-a-codespace)
- [ ] Open the **"AI Models and Keys"** Loop page → find **your chapter**: [Houston](https://m365cpi70459279.sharepoint.com/:fl:/g/contentstorage/CSP_00c67cf6-0806-4c5c-a926-bc5c9ae9cc1c/IQBxO-9QtnsR7y5R1fZ18_1Ab-JFup7kb_wpC3RBm2p49Y?) | [NYC](https://m365cpi70459279.sharepoint.com/:fl:/g/contentstorage/CSP_00c67cf6-0806-4c5c-a926-bc5c9ae9cc1c/IQAuga7cZNqHRbdkVM32pMgpAUloxraJo1WLRaBb0VJl1Fg?e=vdcdUf&) | [Tampa  Florida](https://m365cpi70459279.sharepoint.com/:fl:/g/contentstorage/CSP_00c67cf6-0806-4c5c-a926-bc5c9ae9cc1c/IQDDGojtFNzpTquvqXcp3er8AaEcsHp_m01ouMdFNCAx2kE?e=OvQDw5&%3D) | [Raleigh, NCA/DMV](https://m365cpi70459279.sharepoint.com/:fl:/g/contentstorage/CSP_00c67cf6-0806-4c5c-a926-bc5c9ae9cc1c/IQC1s7BJ9VSxSbhH0qQY4ipsAZQc4eRiiopjDmZsu_mKpzk?e=0OWbfS&%3D) 
- [ ] **Part 1:** Add the BSMP Azure model to Copilot and select it in the model dropdown 👉 see detail on [BSMP AI Models](https://bsmp-coders.github.io/#/2026/program/byok_azure_models_setup?id=%f0%9f%a7%a9-part-1-add-the-model-to-github-copilot)
- [ ] **Part 2:** `cp .env-sample .env` and paste from the **AI Models and Keys** loop page the endpoint, key, and models 👉 see details on [BSMP .env file](https://bsmp-coders.github.io/#/2026/program/byok_azure_models_setup?id=%f0%9f%93%84-part-2-create-your-env-file)
- [ ] (when your finished) save your changes by commit and pushing your changes 👉 see how to [commit and push changes](https://bsmp-coders.github.io/#/students/Getting-Started?id=_4-submitting-your-homework-assignment-how-to-commit-and-push-changes)


### Step 1 — Open Copilot Chat

In Codespaces (VS Code in your browser), open the **Copilot Chat** panel:

- Click the **Copilot chat icon** in the top toolbar (or the sidebar), **or**
- Press **`Ctrl`+`Alt`+`I`** (Windows/Linux) / **`Cmd`+`Ctrl`+`I`** (Mac).

A chat box opens on the side. That's where you'll talk to Copilot. 🎉

### Step 2 — Start the conversation

Copy a prompt like this into the chat to get going:

```text
I'm a high school student in a Coding & AI program. For my final project I need to build a
web or AI app (chatbot, dashboard, agent, or game) and present it in a 5-minute video.

Ask me 3–4 quick questions about my interests, then suggest 3 project ideas that match.
```

Then **actually answer** the questions it asks. The more real you are about what you like
(sports, music, mental health, gaming, your neighborhood, a family business…), the better the ideas.

### Step 3 — Dig into your favorite idea

Pick the idea you like most and keep the conversation going. Try prompts like:

```text
I like idea #2. What would a simple first version look like that I could actually build?
```

```text
What's one AI feature (like AI chat, image generation, or a data insight) that would make
this idea cool but still doable?
```

> 💬 **Anti-vibe-coding tip:** if Copilot says something you don't understand, *ask it to explain*.
> "What do you mean by an AI agent?" is a great question. Understanding beats copying.

---

## ✨ Create: Save your idea to a file

Now have Copilot help you write it down so you don't lose it.

1. Ask Copilot in chat:

   ```text
   Create a file called my_project_idea.md that summarizes the project idea we landed on.
   Include: the project name, what it does, who it's for, and the one AI feature it will use.
   ```

2. Look at the file Copilot creates. **Read it** — does it actually match your idea? If not, tell
   Copilot what to change ("make it about tracking litter in my local park instead").
3. Make it yours: add a sentence in your own words about *why this idea matters to you*.

That's your first artifact for the capstone. 🙌

---

## 🧭 Need Inspiration? Idea Categories

Not sure what excites you? Skim these themes from past students and pick one to explore with Copilot:

- 🧠 **Learning & Growth** — AI tutors, study trackers, scholarship/internship finders
- 🧘 **Mental Health & Support** — mood-tracking chatbots, friendly AI companions
- 🌍 **Social Good & Environment** — carbon-footprint trackers, litter reporters, safety alerts
- 💰 **Finance & Productivity** — teen budgeting coaches, to-do and reminder apps
- 🏥 **Health & Accessibility** — sign-language-to-text, medicine reminders, health monitors
- 🎨 **Creative AI** — video summarizers, fake-news detectors, image tools
- 🎮 **Games & Entertainment** — simple browser games, a "Jarvis"-style assistant
- 🏠 **Community & Family** — a site for a family business, neighborhood helpers

> 🌟 Theme to keep in mind: **AI as a force for good** — solving a real problem you or your community
> care about.

---

## ✅ You're Done When…

- [ ] You opened Copilot Chat and had a back-and-forth conversation about ideas
- [ ] You have a `my_project_idea.md` file with your idea in it
- [ ] You added one sentence in *your own words* about why the idea matters to you
- [ ] save your changes by commit and pushing your changes 👉 see how to [commit and push changes](https://bsmp-coders.github.io/#/students/Getting-Started?id=_4-submitting-your-homework-assignment-how-to-commit-and-push-changes)

Keep this file — you'll build on it as your **Final Video Project** takes shape.

---

## 📎 Reminder: The Final Project

Your capstone is a **5-minute video** showing a working **web or AI app** you built with **Python +
GitHub Copilot** in **Codespaces**, using at least one **AI capability** from class.

📖 Full details, requirements, and prizes:
**[🚀 Final Video Project →](https://bsmp-coders.github.io/#/2026/program/final_projects)**
